sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.35 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: (K.\,Ross). Suppose that a group$G$admits two topologies$$and$$yielding locally compact topological groups$G_{ }$and$G_{ }$. If the sets of closed subgroups in$G_{ }$and$G_{ }$are the same, does it follow that$G_{ }$and$G_{ }$are topologically isomorphic? { Yu.\,N.\,Mukhin} Not always (A.\,I.\,Moskalenko, Ukrain. Math.~J., { 30} (1978), 199--201).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
